articles.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
articles.summary: Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks. [...]

articles.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
articles.summary: Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software. [...]

articles.title: Another Russian dairy company reportedly disrupted by cyberattack
articles.summary: A dairy products manufacturer in Russia's republic of Bashkortostan is the latest such company to have its operations snarled by a cyberattack.

articles.title: The Four Elevations of Effective Fraud Prevention
articles.summary: Fraudsters don't attack just one transaction. They target accounts, platforms, and entire ecosystems. IPQS explains the four elevations of fraud prevention and why broader visibility improves fraud detection. [...]

articles.summary: It’s dumb out there again. articles.title: Ukraine's state postal operator reports app disruption after cyberattack
articles.summary: Ukraine's state-owned postal operator said it was experiencing disruptions to some of its app services due to a suspected cyberattack, but did not say who was behind it.

articles.title: Russia used Cellebrite phone-hacking tool to crack down on dissident after firm cut off country
articles.summary: The continued use of the powerful data extraction product soon after the company in March 2021 said it would stop working with Russia suggests the firm has been unable to pull back its technology from authoritarian government customers, researchers say.

articles.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
articles.summary: Account takeover attacks continue to challenge security teams because attackers often operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. articles.title: Interesting Paper Exploring Prompt Injection
articles.summary: This is a fascinating explotation of how LLMs fall for prompt injection attacks. It turns out that they learn to recognize the style of text in different role/instruction blocks, and not just the tags. articles.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
